Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, Windows Registry
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Application Log Content, Windows Registry Key Modification

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker initiates the campaign by sending targeted phishing emails with malicious attachments labeled 'SampleCheck5000' to energy sector employees. These emails exploit human curiosity and trust, tricking recipients into executing the attached downloader, which establishes the initial foothold in the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The downloader leverages a compromised third-party application's public-facing interface to deploy the Solar backdoor. By injecting malicious code into legitimate application logs, the attacker gains execution privileges, allowing the backdoor to establish persistence within the energy infrastructure's control systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-521: Hardware Design Specifications Are Altered
stage.description: The Solar backdoor modifies Windows registry keys to manipulate SCADA systems' print processors, enabling unauthorized command execution. This compromise allows attackers to destabilize or shut down critical power grid components, creating physical disruptions in energy operations.

Scenario.summary: The Outer Space group executed a multi-stage attack targeting energy infrastructure, beginning with phishing emails to deliver malware, followed by exploitation of application logs to establish persistence, and culminating in registry-based manipulation of SCADA systems to disrupt power grid operations.
